rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. VS. CECILEY MCGRIGER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Thursday, August 29, 2024, Line 2. PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. The levying officer is directed to release the $446.41 and any other earnings held to the judgment creditor for payment on the judgment. The levying officer shall withhold $200/month from defendant's earnings for payment on the judgment. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. If the court adopts the tentative ruling, it will execute a judicial council form of order reflecting the ruling. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD JAMES MCDOUGALL VS. LYFT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Depositions Of Google'S Pmq Of Promotions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Thursday, August 29, 2024, Line 3. DEFENDANT SUSAN MARQUEZ's Motion To Compel Depositions Of Google'S Pmq Of Promotions. The judge pro tem's report and recommendation is adopted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and attaches a copy of the report and recommendation and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Thursday, August 29, 2024, Line 10. 1 - DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO CLEARING SERVICES, LLC's Motion On Its Petition To Confirm Finra Arbitration Award Against Joseph Seidler. rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH SEIDLER ET AL VS. WELLS FARGO CLEARING SERVICES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Thursday, August 29, 2024, Line 10. 2 - PLAINTIFF'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD. Petitioner Joseph Seidler's ("Seidler") petition to vacate arbitration award is denied. Seidler argues that the arbitration proceedings were unconscionable and the award was procured by undue means. (CCP 1286.2(a)(1) [award procured by undue means]. Seidler must show error/undue means and that he was "prejudiced by the alleged corruption, fraud or undue means." (Comerica Bank v. Howsam (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 790, 826.) (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ADAM NUNZIATO, AN INDIVIDUAL VS. ROBERT J. KORMAN, AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND TRUSTEE OF ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Thursday, August 29, 2024, Line 12. 2 - DEFENDANT ROBERT J. KORMAN, AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND TRUSTEE OF THE KORMAN FAMILY IRREVOCABLE TRUST, AND NANCY E. RYTI's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendants Robert Korman and Nancy Ryti's demurrer is overruled. The motion is untimely. On September 6, 2023, Judge Kahn ordered defendants to answer the complaint by September 29, 2023. Instead of doing so, defendants moved to disqualify Judge Kahn and moved for reconsideration of Judge Kahn's 9/6/23 order. Both motions were denied. The court declines to exercise its discretion to hear this motion well after the legislature and the court permitted. (See 9/6/23 Order; CCP 430.40(a) [a demurrer may be brought "within 30 days after service of the complaint"].) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ADAM NUNZIATO, AN INDIVIDUAL VS. ROBERT J. KORMAN, AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND TRUSTEE OF ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Thursday, August 29, 2024, Line 12. 1 - DEFENDANT ROBERT J. KORMAN, AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND TRUSTEE OF THE KORMAN FAMILY IRREVOCABLE TRUST, AND NANCY E. RYTI's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendants Robert Korman and Nancy Ryti's motion to strike is denied. The motion is untimely. On September 6, 2023, Judge Kahn ordered defendants to answer the complaint by September 29, 2023. Instead of doing so, defendants moved to disqualify Judge Kahn and moved for reconsideration of Judge Kahn's 9/6/23 order. Both motions were denied. The court declines to exercise its discretion to hear this motion well after the legislature and the court permitted. (See 9/6/23 Order; CCP 435(b)(1) [a motion to strike may be brought "within the time allowed to respond to a pleading"].) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.
